unit.scope: Server vitest 1351/1351 (106 files), incl. the NEW ws-heartbeat.test.ts (17): createHeartbeatMonitor (terminate only after consecutive missed pongs; tolerance default 2; never terminate while ponged; late-pong-within-tolerance; idempotent-once-dead; custom maxMissedPongs=1), resolveHeartbeatMs (default, valid, floor-clamp, ceiling-clamp, non-numeric/non-positive fallback), startWsHeartbeat (pings each tick; terminate exactly once incl. when terminate() throws + readyState stays OPEN; survives while peer pongs; self-clean on readyState!=OPEN; stop() clears interval+listener idempotently; no-op on missing/partial raw). Client vitest also green (doc-sync gains the ws-heartbeat token; architecture.md updated). tsc --noEmit clean; oxlint exit 0.

e2e.scope: Playwright (1 spec, real Chromium) terminal-heartbeat-keepalive.spec.ts against an isolated prod stack (node dist/index.js, temp USERPROFILE/HOME, SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local, 127.0.0.1:4847) booted with SHIPWRIGHT_TERMINAL_WS_HEARTBEAT_MS=3000. AC-5: open a task terminal, await ready{role:writer}, hold the healthy tab past the dead-socket reap deadline (~10s = >2 intervals; dead would reap ~6s), then assert the terminal WS received NO close frame, exactly one terminal WS opened (no reap->reconnect churn), data-ws-ready=true, data-role=writer, and the read-only banner count is 0. Proves the heartbeat does not falsely reap a ponging connection.

design_fidelity.scope: No UI/visual change — server-side WS control-frame keepalive; browser auto-pongs, no client/** source change.

smoke.scope: Covered by the F0.5 web E2E against the live isolated stack (terminal WS reaches ready and survives the heartbeat).

surface_verification.justification: Diff touches a WS handler consumed by the UI (Backend-affects-Frontend rule => surface=web mandatory). The reap path itself is fully unit-tested deterministically; the web surface verifies the complementary AC-5 (a healthy, ponging tab is NOT reaped) against a real-Chromium isolated stack with a 3s heartbeat.

behaviors.behavior: Monitor reaps only after consecutive missed pongs (tolerance)
Monitor never reaps while a pong arrives each interval
behaviors.evidence: ws-heartbeat.test.ts 'never terminates while a pong arrives' — PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Wiring pings each tick and terminates a dead socket exactly once (incl. terminate() throws)
behaviors.evidence: ws-heartbeat.test.ts startWsHeartbeat exactly-once + throw cases — PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Self-clean when readyState != OPEN; stop() clears interval+listener idempotently
Capability-guard no-op on missing/partial raw socket
A healthy terminal WS is not reaped across multiple heartbeat intervals (AC-5)
Dead writer reaped -> surviving tab auto-promoted end-to-end over a REAL half-open TCP from a browser
behaviors.reason: There is no deterministic way to half-open a TCP from a browser/Playwright; the reap+promote path is proven by the unit-tested terminate-once behavior plus the existing pty-manager detach/reader-promotion tests.
